title: geranium krameri var. chejuense
intro: Geranium krameri var. chejuense, also known as Cheju geranium or inselberg geranium, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is native to the Cheju Island in South Korea and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Geranium krameri var. chejuense include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin disorders and respiratory issues.
